<file>
00001| """
00002| Tuya IoT Platform Integration
00003| Fetches real-time data from multiple Tuya smart plugs
00004| """
00005| 
00006| from tuya_connector import TuyaOpenAPI
00007| import requests
00008| import time
00009| from datetime import datetime
00010| 
00011| # ==================== TUYA CREDENTIALS ====================
00012| # Replace these with your actual Tuya IoT Platform credentials
00013| 
00014| ACCESS_ID = "your_access_id_here"          # From Tuya IoT Platform
00015| ACCESS_KEY = "your_access_key_here"        # From Tuya IoT Platform
00016| API_ENDPOINT = "https://openapi.tuyain.com"  # For India region
00017| # Other regions:
00018| # US: https://openapi.tuyaus.com
00019| # EU: https://openapi.tuyaeu.com
00020| # China: https://openapi.tuyacn.com
00021| 
00022| # Your device IDs (get from Tuya IoT Platform -> Devices tab)
00023| DEVICE_IDS = [
00024|     "device_id_1",  # TV Smart Plug
00025|     "device_id_2",  # Router Smart Plug
00026|     "device_id_3",  # Charger Smart Plug
00027|     "device_id_4",  # Refrigerator Smart Plug
00028| ]
00029| 
00030| # Device name mapping
00031| DEVICE_NAMES = {
00032|     "device_id_1": "TV",
00033|     "device_id_2": "Router",
00034|     "device_id_3": "Charger",
00035|     "device_id_4": "Refrigerator"
00036| }
00037| 
00038| # Your Flask backend URL
00039| BACKEND_URL = "http://localhost:5000"
00040| 
00041| # ==================== TUYA API CONNECTION ====================
00042| 
00043| def connect_to_tuya():
00044|     """
00045|     Connect to Tuya IoT Platform using your credentials
00046|     """
00047|     openapi = TuyaOpenAPI(API_ENDPOINT, ACCESS_ID, ACCESS_KEY)
00048|     openapi.connect()
00049|     print("✅ Connected to Tuya IoT Platform")
00050|     return openapi
00051| 
00052| # ==================== FETCH DEVICE DATA ====================
00053| 
00054| def get_device_status(openapi, device_id):
00055|     """
00056|     Get current status and power consumption from a single device
00057|     
00058|     Returns data like:
00059|     {
00060|         "switch_1": True/False,
00061|         "cur_power": 850,  # Current power in watts (multiply by 10)
00062|         "cur_voltage": 2300,  # Voltage in 0.1V (230.0V)
00063|         "cur_current": 400  # Current in mA (0.4A)
00064|     }
00065|     """
00066|     try:
00067|         response = openapi.get(f"/v1.0/devices/{device_id}/status")
00068|         
00069|         if response['success']:
00070|             status_data = {}
00071|             for item in response['result']:
00072|                 status_data[item['code']] = item['value']
00073|             return status_data
00074|         else:
00075|             print(f"❌ Error fetching data for device {device_id}: {response}")
00076|             return None
00077|             
00078|     except Exception as e:
00079|         print(f"❌ Exception for device {device_id}: {e}")
00080|         return None
00081| 
00082| def process_device_data(device_id, status_data):
00083|     """
00084|     Convert Tuya device data to EcoTrack format
00085|     """
00086|     device_name = DEVICE_NAMES.get(device_id, f"Device_{device_id[:8]}")
00087|     
00088|     # Extract power data (Tuya returns power in 0.1W, so divide by 10)
00089|     cur_power = status_data.get('cur_power', 0) / 10  # Convert to watts
00090|     cur_voltage = status_data.get('cur_voltage', 0) / 10  # Convert to volts
00091|     switch_status = status_data.get('switch_1', False)
00092|     
00093|     # Determine mode
00094|     if not switch_status:
00095|         mode = "Idle"
00096|     elif cur_power < 5:
00097|         mode = "Standby"
00098|     else:
00099|         mode = "ON"
00100|     
00101|     return {
00102|         "device_name": device_name,
00103|         "mode": mode,
00104|         "voltage": round(cur_voltage, 2),
00105|         "power_watts": round(cur_power, 2),
00106|         "timestamp": datetime.now().isoformat()
00107|     }
00108| 
00109| def send_to_backend(data):
00110|     """
00111|     Send data to Flask backend API
00112|     """
00113|     try:
00114|         response = requests.post(
00115|             f"{BACKEND_URL}/api/electricity/add",
00116|             json=data
00117|         )
00118|         if response.status_code == 201:
00119|             print(f"✅ Sent data for {data['device_name']}: {data['power_watts']}W")
00120|         else:
00121|             print(f"❌ Failed to send data: {response.status_code}")
00122|     except Exception as e:
00123|         print(f"❌ Error sending data: {e}")
00124| 
00125| # ==================== MAIN LOOP ====================
00126| 
00127| def fetch_all_devices(openapi):
00128|     """
00129|     Fetch data from all registered Tuya devices
00130|     """
00131|     print(f"\n🔄 Fetching data from {len(DEVICE_IDS)} devices...")
00132|     
00133|     for device_id in DEVICE_IDS:
00134|         status_data = get_device_status(openapi, device_id)
00135|         
00136|         if status_data:
00137|             processed_data = process_device_data(device_id, status_data)
00138|             send_to_backend(processed_data)
00139|         
00140|         time.sleep(0.5)  # Small delay between requests
00141| 
00142| def main():
00143|     """
00144|     Main function - continuously fetch data from Tuya devices
00145|     """
00146|     print("🚀 Starting Tuya IoT Integration...")
00147|     print(f"📡 Backend URL: {BACKEND_URL}")
00148|     print(f"🔌 Monitoring {len(DEVICE_IDS)} devices\n")
00149|     
00150|     # Connect to Tuya
00151|     openapi = connect_to_tuya()
00152|     
00153|     # Continuous monitoring loop
00154|     update_interval = 60  # Fetch data every 60 seconds
00155|     
00156|     try:
00157|         while True:
00158|             fetch_all_devices(openapi)
00159|             print(f"⏳ Waiting {update_interval} seconds before next update...\n")
00160|             time.sleep(update_interval)
00161|     
00162|     except KeyboardInterrupt:
00163|         print("\n🛑 Stopping Tuya integration...")
00164| 
00165| if __name__ == "__main__":
00166|     main()
00167| 

(End of file - total 167 lines)

metadata.preview:
"""
Tuya IoT Platform Integration
Fetches real-time data from multiple Tuya smart plugs
"""

from tuya_connector import TuyaOpenAPI
import requests
import time
from datetime import datetime

# ==================== TUYA CREDENTIALS ====================
# Replace these with your actual Tuya IoT Platform credentials

ACCESS_ID = "your_access_id_here"          # From Tuya IoT Platform
ACCESS_KEY = "your_access_key_here"        # From Tuya IoT Platform
API_ENDPOINT = "https://openapi.tuyain.com"  # For India region
# Other regions:
# US: https://openapi.tuyaus.com
# EU: https://openapi.tuyaeu.com
# China: https://openapi.tuyacn.com
